item.title: Add skill system section to docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
item.intent: A contributor or evaluator reading ARCHITECTURE.md must understand the full system — the skill system is a core architectural layer that is currently undocumented there.
item.description: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md is nearly production-ready and covers platform, blueprints, security, deployment, modules, and architectural decisions. But the skill system — a core runtime layer — has no dedicated section. Skills are documented individually in configs/skills/*/SKILL.md and configs/skills/*/config.yaml but the architectural pattern (declarative skill definition, cron trigger, prompt templates, boundary enforcement, approval gates) is never explained as a system. Add a 'Layer 3: Skills' section (or 'The Skill System') to ARCHITECTURE.md between the Blueprint and Security sections. Source content from: the 6 existing SKILL.md files (email-digest, investor-update, market-scan, meal-plan, morning-brief, schedule-guard) for the pattern, and the 6 config.yaml files for the schema (schedule, model, dependencies, approval, boundaries). The section should explain: what a skill is, the skill lifecycle (trigger → prompt → execute → boundary check → approval gate), the config.yaml schema, and how skills compose with blueprints.
steps: Read docs/ARCHITECTURE.md to understand the current structure and find the right insertion point (after Layer 2: Blueprints, before Security Architecture)
Read configs/skills/email-digest/SKILL.md and configs/skills/email-digest/config.yaml as the reference skill implementation
Read configs/skills/morning-brief/SKILL.md and configs/skills/morning-brief/config.yaml for a second pattern example
Read at least 2 more configs/skills/*/config.yaml files to confirm the schema is consistent across skills

Add a new section to docs/ARCHITECTURE.md covering: what a skill is (declarative capability definition), skill lifecycle (trigger → prompt template → model execution → boundary enforcement → approval gate if configured), config.yaml schema reference (schedule, model, dependencies, approval, boundaries fields), how blueprints compose skills (blueprint YAML references skill names), skill isolation and boundary enforcement
Ensure the new section follows the same tone and depth as the existing Blueprint and Platform sections
acceptanceCriteria: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md contains a skill system section explaining: what skills are, the skill lifecycle, config.yaml schema, blueprint-skill composition, and boundary enforcement
The section is sourced from existing SKILL.md and config.yaml files — no invented claims
The section is positioned logically within the document (between blueprints and security, or after blueprints)
Existing content in ARCHITECTURE.md is not altered beyond the insertion
